note: The FBI takes reported crime data and adjusts it for gaps and underreporting to provide consistent long-term estimates over time. That means it may be different to reported crime figures.
title: Violent crime rates in the United States
subtitle: Estimated rates per 100,000 people. [Violent crime](#dod:violent-crime) includes four offenses: murder and non-negligent manslaughter, rape, [robbery](#dod:robbery), and [aggravated assault](#dod:aggravated-assault). Data for rape is not shown due to the lack of comparable data over time.

display.name: Robbery (taking something through force or violence)
